prestazioni: Engine swap to a Fiat-Lancia Twin-Cam 2.0L 16V (engine code: 831D1.000) with forged internals: JE 87mm forged pistons, Cunningham conrods, and a billet crankshaft, paired with a custom dry-sump system by Aviaid for sustained high-RPM reliability (target: 220+ HP at 8,500 RPM).

Forced induction via a Garrett GT2860RS turbocharger with a custom 3.5-inch stainless steel exhaust manifold by Tubistyle, mated to a GReddy Type-S intercooler and a custom 3-inch downpipe with a Vibrant 1799 ultra-quiet resonator for backpressure optimization.

Chassis-specific suspension overhaul: Ohlins Road & Track DFV coilovers with custom valving for the Montecarlo’s unique mid-engine layout, paired with polyurethane bushings from SuperPro (part #SPF1050K) and a custom-fabricated strut tower brace by Autocadabra to eliminate chassis flex under lateral load.
estetica: Full carbon fiber widebody conversion using a custom Rocket Bunny-inspired kit by Liberty Walk, adapted for the Montecarlo’s dimensions (fenders extended by 50mm, rear quarter panels widened by 60mm) with integrated canards and a ducktail spoiler for aerodynamic balance.

Custom 17x9.5-inch front and 17x11-inch rear wheels by Work Meister S1 3P (ET20 front, ET15 rear) wrapped in Michelin Pilot Sport Cup 2 tires (245/40/17 front, 295/35/17 rear), paired with Brembo GT 6-piston calipers and 355mm two-piece rotors up front, 330mm at the rear.

Interior stripped and reupholstered with a full Alcantara package by Recaro (CS-E seats with custom Lancia script embroidery), a MOMO Prototipo steering wheel, and a digital dash cluster by AIM MXS Strada with integrated data logging for track telemetry.
